title: The people behind the papers - Brian Eames.
abstract: Differentiation into the three main cell types of the vertebrate skeleton - immature chondrocytes, mature chondrocytes and osteoblasts - is influenced by two key transcription factors, Sox9 and Runx2. In a new paper in Development, Gomez-Picos et al. perform detailed analysis on the wider skeletal gene regulatory network in mouse embryos to understand how distinct Sox9- and Runx2-containing subnetworks influence skeletal cell fate. We spoke to corresponding author Brian Eames, Professor at the University of Saskatchewan, Canada, to find out more about this project he conducted with former lab members and co-first authors Patsy Gomez-Picos, Katie Ovens and Amir Ashique.
